The foundation's EIN is 300221345 and their contact email is [info@shareasmileusa.org](mailto:info@shareasmileusa.org).

items.title: Wall Lake View Auburn Community School Foundation
items.description: The Wall Lake View Auburn Community School Foundation is located in Lake View, IA, USA, and donates to nonprofits located in Iowa. Despite its active engagement in the state, it appears that the foundation does not support any known causes, as evidenced by its limited scope of donations. With a total of only four donations made exclusively within Iowa, the foundation has not expanded its philanthropic efforts beyond this single state. Furthermore, the foundation has only contributed to one unique state, indicating a very narrow focus. This emphasis on local giving, while beneficial to the immediate community, raises questions about the foundation's overall mission and its commitment to addressing broader social issues or supporting diverse causes across the country.

items.title: Ann Sputh Family Foundation INC
items.description: The Ann Sputh Family Foundation Inc, based in Tuscaloosa, AL, primarily donates to nonprofit organizations focused on religious, educational, and social services. A significant portion of its funding supports Christian organizations that promote spiritual growth and community worship. The foundation also backs universities, demonstrating a commitment to higher education. In addition, it contributes to human service organizations that offer essential social services such as counseling for at-risk populations and supports specialty hospitals, highlighting its focus on healthcare. The foundation engages in international relief efforts, aiding poverty alleviation and disaster response in developing countries. Furthermore, it supports services for children and youth, homeless shelters, and centers for developmentally disabled individuals, reflecting a broad commitment to diverse community welfare. Its influence is particularly notable in states like Illinois, Texas, and Indiana.

items.title: The Carter Family Charitable Foundation INC
items.description: The Carter Family Foundation is a 501(c)(3) organization that supports people with intellectual disabilities, autism, and their families through education and resource programs. The foundation was established by Aeisha Carter, who has over 35 years of experience working with these populations. The foundation's mission is to provide opportunities for self-sufficiency and offers various programs and initiatives, including housing and community support services. The foundation also provides resources and education to help families and individuals affected by intellectual disabilities and autism.

items.title: Ruahmah J Hutchings Fund Irrev
items.description: The Ruahmah J Hutchings Fund Irrev is located in Howell, MI, USA, and it usually donates to organizations that provide scholarships and student financial aid. This category encompasses nonprofits that assist students demonstrating financial need, ensuring they receive the necessary financial assistance to cover their educational and living expenses while pursuing their studies. By focusing on scholarships, the foundation aims to alleviate the financial burdens faced by students, enabling them to achieve their academic goals and improve their future prospects. The foundation has been particularly active in Michigan, where it has made all of its donations. This localized focus suggests a commitment to supporting the educational aspirations of students within the state, helping to foster a more educated community. Overall, the foundation's contributions reflect a strong dedication to empowering students through educational support.

items.title: Ted And Jane Von Voigtlander Foundation
items.description: The Ted and Jane Von Voigtlander Foundation, based in Howell, MI, supports various nonprofit organizations centered on community support and education. It primarily funds human service organizations that provide essential social services, enhancing community well-being. The foundation also aids scholarship and financial aid organizations to help students access educational opportunities. Additionally, it invests in undergraduate colleges to promote higher education and supports children's museums that offer interactive learning experiences. Other key areas of focus include family counseling services, environmental education initiatives, and food banks, addressing critical issues like mental health, ecological awareness, and hunger. The foundation's primary activity is in Michigan, underscoring its commitment to local philanthropic efforts aimed at strengthening and uplifting the community.

items.title: John W & Barbara A Dant Charitable Foundation
items.description: The John W. & Barbara A. Dant Charitable Foundation, based in Murfreesboro, TN, primarily donates to nonprofits focused on rehabilitation and support services. It emphasizes organizations that aid offenders through counseling and job-seeking assistance, aiming to help individuals reintegrate into society post-incarceration. This commitment reflects the foundation's goal of reducing recidivism and fostering social reintegration. The foundation also supports services for developmentally disabled individuals, enhancing their quality of life. Additionally, it backs specific healthcare initiatives, contributing to the overall health of the communities it serves. Most of the foundation's activities and donations are concentrated in Tennessee, with notable contributions also made to Indiana and Illinois, showcasing its regional impact.

items.title: The Roklen Foundation
items.description: The Roklen Foundation is located in Los Angeles, CA, and donates to nonprofits located in various states across the United States. Despite being active in 14 unique states, the foundation's contributions do not appear to support any widely recognized causes or initiatives. This suggests that their focus may be on smaller, perhaps less publicized organizations rather than on well-known charitable causes that typically attract significant attention and funding. The top states receiving donations include New York and California, among others, yet the absence of a clear alignment with major causes raises questions about the foundation's strategic philanthropic goals. Overall, while the foundation demonstrates a commitment to giving, the lack of support for known causes may indicate a distinct approach to philanthropy that prioritizes lesser-known entities.

items.title: The Berghorst Foundation INC
items.description: The Berghorst Foundation Inc, based in Wilmington, DE, donates to various nonprofit organizations with a focus on youth development and the arts. A significant portion of its funding supports programs that provide recreational and educational opportunities for children and young adults, helping them become responsible citizens. The foundation also backs theater organizations, enhancing cultural enrichment through plays. Moreover, it invests in human service organizations that deliver social services to families in need and provides scholarships for financially challenged students. Support extends to arts education, primary and elementary schools, and community service clubs, highlighting a commitment to education and community improvement. The foundation is particularly active in states like Illinois and Florida, areas with significant nonprofit engagement. Overall, the Berghorst Foundation aims to nurture youth, promote the arts, and enhance community services.

items.title: Mw Family Foundation INC
items.description: The Mw Family Foundation, based in Lakewood, NJ, donates to diverse nonprofit organizations, primarily focusing on religious and humanitarian causes. A significant portion of their contributions supports Judaism, emphasizing Jewish traditions and community values. They also aid human service organizations that provide essential social services, including family counseling and employment assistance. Their philanthropic efforts extend internationally, aiming to alleviate poverty and enhance welfare in developing countries, particularly during crises. The foundation collaborates with food banks and pantries to ensure vulnerable populations have access to nutrition. Additionally, they support rehabilitative care for individuals with disabilities and promote international understanding, reflecting a commitment to global awareness and cooperation. Active primarily in New York and New Jersey, the foundation balances a strong local focus with broader national and international outreach.

items.title: Heavenly Treasure
items.description: The Heavenly Treasure, based in Oak Ridge, TN, donates to various nonprofit organizations addressing community needs, with a strong focus on Roman Catholic entities, including churches and educational programs that promote faith and outreach. It also supports group homes for individuals with disabilities, ensuring they receive essential care in a nurturing environment. Recognizing the value of education, the foundation contributes to secondary and high schools that prepare students for further education or employment. The foundation primarily operates in Tennessee, where it has made the most donations, followed by Pennsylvania and California, showcasing its commitment to local and regional nonprofits that cater to religious support, educational development, and the care of vulnerable populations.

items.title: George W & Elizabeth W Kelly Foundation
items.description: The George W. & Elizabeth W. Kelly Foundation, based in Lewis Center, OH, donates to nonprofits that promote the independence of specific populations, such as individuals with disabilities and those facing socioeconomic challenges. It supports organizations that fundraise for educational institutions, enhancing educational opportunities. The foundation also provides scholarships and financial aid to students in need for their educational and living expenses. Additionally, it contributes to historical organizations that promote awareness and appreciation of history, enriching cultural education. The foundation addresses housing needs by supporting organizations that develop subsidized rental housing for low-income families. Primarily active in Ohio, it has made most donations there, with some involvement in New York and Arizona, reflecting a regional focus on local needs and community development.

items.title: L & F Schwartz Foundation Trust
items.description: The L & F Schwartz Foundation Trust is located in Brooklyn, NY, and donates to nonprofits located in a limited number of areas. Notably, the foundation's donation activity is concentrated primarily in just two states: New York and New Jersey. With a total of only 11 donations, the foundation's contributions appear to be minimal and lack a broader commitment to known causes or extensive outreach. The focus on such a small geographic area raises questions about the foundation's overall impact and its alignment with widely recognized charitable initiatives. The limited scope of donations suggests that the foundation may not be prioritizing well-known causes or organizations, instead choosing to operate within a narrow framework. This raises concerns about the effectiveness and reach of its philanthropic efforts, indicating a need for a more diverse strategy in supporting various nonprofit organizations and causes.

items.title: Luchen Foundation
items.description: The Lujiang Foundation is a non-profit organization that was established in 2002 with the purpose of improving the cultural quality of Hsinchu County citizens and promoting social harmony and progress. The foundation supports various educational programs, including reading education, hometown education, environmental protection, gender equality, self-exploration, and national aesthetic education. In addition, the foundation has earned national recognition for its transformative impact on literacy outcomes, with 69% of fourth graders in the United States unable to read at grade level according to the 2024 Nation's Report Card. It has been particularly active in California, demonstrating a strong presence in both urban and rural communities across the nation.

items.title: The Grove Foundation
items.description: The Grove Foundation, based in Los Altos, CA, focuses on supporting nonprofits that promote social equality, civil rights, and community welfare. It particularly funds organizations that enhance the independence of marginalized ethnic groups and those facing socioeconomic challenges. The foundation emphasizes human services, backing multiservice organizations that provide family counseling and employment assistance. It advocates for minority and civil rights, supporting groups that seek legal protections for underrepresented communities. Additionally, the Grove Foundation prioritizes voter education and registration to encourage civic engagement. Its contributions extend to public foundations that offer legal services for the economically disadvantaged and community development projects. While primarily active in California, it also makes significant donations in Washington D.C., New York, and Arizona, highlighting its commitment to fostering social change across various regions.

items.title: Nindra Foundation
items.description: The Nindra Foundation, based in Suffolk, VA, donates to various nonprofits focusing on community well-being and social justice. It primarily supports community clinics providing medical services to low-income residents and organizations dedicated to youth services that aid vulnerable children. The foundation is also involved in civil rights advocacy, funding organizations that protect marginalized groups' rights. Additionally, it contributes to international development, assisting countries in improving their agriculture, health, and education systems. The foundation supports public grantmaking organizations to ensure effective fund allocation to charitable causes. Most of Nindra Foundation's donations are made in Virginia, with significant contributions in Washington, DC, and Indiana. Overall, its efforts reflect a strong commitment to enhancing community health, advocating for rights, and supporting global development initiatives.

items.title: The Foutch Foundation
items.description: The Foutch Foundation is located in Tulsa, OK, USA, and donates to nonprofits located in various states across the country. Notably, it has been most active in just two states: Oklahoma and California, with a total of only two donations recorded. This limited scope suggests that the foundation's philanthropic efforts are not aligned with any widely recognized causes or initiatives. Instead, its contributions appear to be minimal and perhaps more localized, lacking the breadth typically associated with larger foundations that support well-known causes. Consequently, the foundation does not seem to engage significantly with broader social issues or high-impact nonprofit organizations. Its focus remains restricted, raising questions about the overall impact and mission of the foundation in the nonprofit landscape.

items.title: Caleb Development Corporation
items.description: The Caleb Development Corporation is located in Lynn, MA, and it usually donates to nonprofits focused on housing development and management. This foundation primarily supports organizations that build, rehabilitate, and manage rental housing for low-income individuals and families, older adults, and people with disabilities. By facilitating affordable housing options, such as lower-priced housing and payment plans, the foundation plays a crucial role in enhancing the living conditions of marginalized communities. Its philanthropic efforts are concentrated in Massachusetts, where it has made a significant impact through its donations. The foundation’s commitment to improving housing accessibility reflects a dedication to social equity and community development, addressing the pressing needs of those who may struggle to secure safe and affordable housing.

items.title: The Clark Nexsen Foundation INC
items.description: The Clark Nexsen Foundation Inc., based in Virginia Beach, VA, supports a wide range of nonprofit organizations, focusing primarily on education, food security, and community services. A substantial part of its funding is directed towards educational initiatives, demonstrating a commitment to improving educational opportunities. The foundation also assists food banks and pantries that combat hunger by providing essential food supplies. In addition, it funds organizations that raise money for health causes, arts and culture programs, and housing development for low-income individuals. The foundation supports women's service clubs dedicated to community enhancement and funds historical organizations that promote awareness of history. Active mainly in Virginia and North Carolina, it showcases a strong regional commitment while engaging with nonprofits across 30 states. With contributions spanning 14 categories, the foundation’s philanthropic efforts are both extensive and impactful.

items.title: The Jemf Foundation
items.description: The JEM Foundation is a non-profit organization dedicated to preventing suicide and supporting individuals and families affected by mental health issues. The foundation was established in memory of Jacob Edward Machovsky, who passed away from suicide on January 11, 2016. The foundation's mission is to end youth suicide and improve the lives of those struggling with mental illness. The JEM Foundation provides various services and programs, including: * Free depression assessment and prevention * Mental health assistance initiative fund * Parent/caregiver support group * Youth depression, anxiety, eating disorders, and mental health first aid * Relational bullying insurance action * Jake's Law insurance parity initiative * Animal therapy at Jacob's Ranch The foundation also offers educational resources, training, and speaking engagements to help individuals and communities understand and address mental health issues. Donations to the JEM Foundation can be made to support their mission.

items.title: Joachim & Vicki Peters Foundation
items.description: The Joachim & Vicki Peters Foundation, based in Palatine, IL, primarily supports nonprofits that enhance cultural and educational opportunities, particularly in the performing arts. This focus fosters creativity and community engagement. The foundation also provides financial assistance to organizations that raise funds for specific educational institutions, highlighting its commitment to education and institutional development. Active mainly in Illinois, the foundation has made numerous donations locally, but it also contributes to organizations in New York, Minnesota, and Indiana, showcasing its broader interest in supporting arts and education across various regions. Overall, the foundation's contributions significantly enhance cultural and educational landscapes.

items.title: Herbert & Katherine Kurth Religious
items.description: The Herbert & Katherine Kurth Religious Foundation, based in Brookfield, WI, primarily supports organizations focused on international relief, arts and culture, and food banks. It emphasizes international relief, aiding nonprofits that alleviate poverty in developing countries and enhance health, education, and social well-being, demonstrating a commitment to global humanitarian efforts. The foundation also funds arts and culture initiatives to promote access to visual and performing arts, reflecting its belief in the value of cultural enrichment. Additionally, it addresses food insecurity by donating to food banks and pantries, ensuring immediate nutritional support for those in need. Geographically, the foundation is active in Washington D.C., New York, Wisconsin, and several other states, showcasing a broad reach across the United States in its philanthropic efforts.

items.title: Roy L Parker Family Foundation INC
items.description: The Parker Family Foundation is a non-profit organization that supports arts and education in the Greater Boston area. The foundation emphasizes personal connections and active engagement with organizations it supports, and pursues long-term relationships based on consistent and continuous support. Since its inception in 2001, PFF has made grants totaling over $15 million to organizations that foster artistic exploration and expression, provide broad-based public access to art, and work cooperatively to improve public education. The foundation's trustees are Glenn P. Parker and Faith K. P. Parker.

items.title: Dance Ready INC
items.description: The Dance Ready Project is a foundation that provides tools and programs to reduce dance injury risk and optimize performance for dancers across the lifespan, from students to professionals. The foundation has three core arms: Assessment, Conditioning, and Education. The Dance Ready Score is a baseline fitness assessment specifically designed for dancers that evaluates key physical capacities such as strength, balance, flexibility, and cardiorespiratory fitness. The foundation also provides customized interventions and conditioning programs to help dancers strengthen their weaknesses, enhance their performance, and prevent future injuries. The founders of the Dance Ready Project are Dr. Sheyi Ojofeitimi, a licensed physical therapist and Director of Therapy Services at the Alvin Ailey Dance Foundation, and Janet Doe, the founder, and Chief Technology Officer.

items.title: Fore Aces Family Foundation
items.description: The Fore Aces Family Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, primarily supports nonprofit organizations focused on education, community support, and health services. It mainly funds undergraduate colleges, enhancing educational opportunities for students. The foundation also allocates resources to family services and patient support organizations that assist families during health-related challenges. Additionally, it is involved in housing development, contributing to organizations that provide affordable housing for low-income families. The foundation emphasizes senior services by supporting centers for the elderly and engages in various community initiatives, including business support and educational programs aimed at improving local economies and quality of life. Its donations are particularly concentrated in states like Vermont and Maine, highlighting its strong presence in the northeastern United States.

items.title: The Karen Toffler Charitable Trust
items.description: The Karen Toffler Charitable Trust is a 501c-3 nonprofit organization that supports early-stage medical research, education, and technology to revolutionize medicine and technology. The trust was established by the Toffler family to honor their legacy and continue their work in creating solutions for some of humanity's most intractable challenges. The trust supports innovative and entrepreneurial medical researchers who are taking on important and underfunded challenges, such as neurological diseases. They provide vital early-stage network and funding support to these individuals, connecting them with other funders and scientists to create new ways of thinking and problem-solving. The trust embracces risk and embraces high-risk ideas that often have the highest impact when they succeed. Over the past 8 years, the trust has funded 113 initiatives and supported 12 universities, with a focus on making a bigger impact in the medical field. Your involvement will change humanity for the better, so join them in their mission to revolutionize medicine and technology.

items.title: The Ray And June Bishop Foundation
items.description: The Ray and June Bishop Foundation, based in Springfield, CO, primarily donates to educational nonprofits, focusing on two-year colleges or community colleges. These institutions provide accessible postsecondary education tailored to local community needs. The foundation aims to enhance educational opportunities for those pursuing an Associate in Arts certificate, which can lead to further education or improved job prospects. Most of the foundation's donations are made in Colorado, with some support extending to Kansas, demonstrating a strong commitment to local educational initiatives and workforce development. Overall, the foundation's philanthropic efforts emphasize enhancing educational access and quality in its operational regions.

items.title: Hofstetter Family Foundation INC
items.description: The Hofstetter Family Foundation Inc. is located in Ponte Vedra, FL, USA, and donates to nonprofits located in various states across the country. However, an analysis of their donation patterns reveals that the foundation does not appear to support any widely recognized causes. Despite making donations primarily within Florida, the foundation's contributions lack a clear alignment with specific charitable missions or well-known initiatives. This absence of targeted giving raises questions about the foundation's philanthropic objectives and the impact of its donations on the nonprofit sector. With only two unique states receiving support, it seems that the foundation's focus is rather limited, potentially leaving out a broader array of causes that could benefit from its resources. Thus, while the foundation is active in its local community, its contributions do not seem to address any notable issues or causes that typically engage nonprofit organizations.

items.title: Ecoed Foundation
items.description: The Ecoed Foundation, based in Brookfield, WI, primarily donates to nonprofits centered on environmental conservation, especially water resource management. It supports organizations dedicated to preserving wetlands and coastal areas, protecting water supplies, ensuring quality for public use, agriculture, and industry, and maintaining healthy aquatic habitats like rivers and lakes. The foundation is most active in Washington, D.C., where it has made the majority of its donations, followed by Florida and New York. This geographic focus highlights its commitment to water conservation and environmental stewardship in both urban and coastal regions. Overall, the Ecoed Foundation emphasizes initiatives that promote the sustainable management of natural resources, particularly water, aligning with its mission to foster ecological health and sustainability.

items.title: Guardian Angels Of Naples
items.description: The Guardian Angels of Naples is located in Naples, FL, and it usually donates to nonprofits focused on animal welfare and protection. The foundation prioritizes organizations that ensure humane care and control of animals, primarily through support for animal shelters that address cruelty and provide necessary care. Additionally, they contribute to wildlife preservation efforts, which involve the conservation and management of various wildlife species and their habitats. Their donations also extend to educational initiatives related to these themes, reflecting a broader commitment to promoting awareness and understanding of animal and environmental issues. The foundation is most active in Florida, where it has made the majority of its donations, but it has also reached out to organizations in a few other states, indicating a regional focus with a specific emphasis on animal-related causes.

items.title: Grantors Foundation
items.description: U.S. Business Grants is an organization that helps small businesses find, apply, and receive grants. They offer eligibility instructions, a process FAQs, and a recipients check status page. Grant funding provides financial assistance to help U.S.-based businesses and nonprofit entities, and funds are allocated based on availability, guidelines, and submission of required information. The organization is committed to assisting small businesses find and receive grants, and they provide $0 services for stand applications. They offer grant programs for women, minority-owned businesses, and other specific industries, and the application process is easier to navigate for these demographics. Additionally, they list various government, state, county, and city funded grants available in different areas.

items.title: Park Educational Trust
items.description: The Park Educational Trust is located in Waterloo, IA, USA, and it usually donates to community foundations. These organizations are dedicated to making grants for charitable purposes within specific communities or regions, often utilizing funds from various donors that are held in an endowment. The income generated by this endowment is used to provide financial support to local initiatives and nonprofits. The foundation is particularly active in Iowa, where it has made the majority of its donations. This focus on community foundations indicates a commitment to enhancing local welfare and supporting grassroots efforts that address the unique needs of the community. By channeling resources into community foundations, the Park Educational Trust plays a vital role in fostering positive change and development in its immediate geographic area.

items.title: The Freund Family Foundation
items.description: The Freund Family Foundation is a private philanthropic foundation registered as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit based in Brooklyn, New York, that makes tax-deductible charitable grants to other nonprofit organizations rather than running its own programs. It holds several million dollars in assets (around $4.3 million in its most recent filing) and has funded dozens of grants to a variety of causes, including religious, community, and general charitable organizations across the United States. The foundation is managed by trustees Meir and Bella Freund, who serve voluntarily, and its activities reflect a focus on supporting nonprofits through annual grantmaking.

items.title: The Scrivan Family Foundation
items.description: The Scrivan Family Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, supports a wide array of nonprofit organizations. It primarily focuses on museums dedicated to acquiring, preserving, and exhibiting artifacts in natural sciences and history. The foundation also backs public charities that benefit the community and promote grantmaking initiatives. Emphasizing arts education, it funds organizations that offer informal arts programming and instruction. Health-related nonprofits, particularly those providing general and rehabilitative services, receive funding, along with undergraduate colleges offering bachelor's degrees. Community service clubs that enhance local life through fundraising and projects are also key beneficiaries. Other support areas include recreation, natural resource conservation, family planning, and public health initiatives. The foundation is notably active in Florida, Connecticut, and Maryland, reflecting its commitment to improving community welfare across various sectors.

items.title: The Evalyn O Flory Foundation
items.description: The Evalyn O. Flory Foundation, based in Wenatchee, WA, supports nonprofits focused on developmental support and education for youth, as well as cultural and historical preservation. A significant portion of its donations goes to Developmentally Disabled Centers, highlighting its commitment to individuals with developmental disabilities. The foundation also actively funds preschools, emphasizing early childhood education. The foundation's commitment to supporting Christian nonprofits is evident in its donation patterns, reflecting an interest in fostering spiritual development and community engagement among its beneficiaries. items.title: The Ayuda Foundation
items.description: The Ayuda Foundation is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization based in Guam that provides medical support, human services, and developmental assistance throughout the Pacific region. The foundation was founded in 1996 by Carlotta Leon Guerrero, Dr. Mike Cruz of the Guam Medical Society, and Christine Nilsen of Continental Micronesia (now United Airlines). The organization's mission is to deliver medical care, food, and water to people in the Pacific region, particularly in the Micronesian region. The foundation supports specific causes such as cancer assistance, emergency disaster relief, diabetes education, NGO development, and research support. They also have programs such as medical missions, broken crayon project, service learning, and eye care. The Ayuda Foundation has received donations from partners and sponsors that make a positive impact.

items.title: Ncma Education Research Foundation
items.description: The NCMA Foundation is a non-profit organization that supports education and research in the concrete masonry and hardscape industry. The foundation was established in 1988 and has invested over $2 million in grants to various programs, including research for codes and standards, educational opportunities for architectural and engineering students, and workforce development programs. The foundation is led by a board of trustees comprised of visionary leaders in the industry, and it relies on the support of industry members to continue its efforts. The foundation offers scholarships, grant applications, and various programs to advance the industry and benefit the public interest.

items.title: Cannupa Foundation
items.description: The Cannupa Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, USA, donates to various nonprofit organizations focusing on education, community support, and humanitarian efforts. A significant portion of its contributions goes to universities, underscoring a commitment to higher education. The foundation also supports public libraries and preschools, highlighting its investment in literacy and early childhood education. Additionally, it contributes to international relief organizations, demonstrating a dedication to alleviating poverty and responding to global emergencies. Locally, the foundation aids homeless shelters and food programs, reflecting its commitment to vulnerable populations. Support for community service clubs and philanthropic advocacy organizations further emphasizes its focus on civic engagement. The foundation's contributions span numerous states, with a notable concentration in Connecticut, Washington D.C., and California.

items.title: Callaghan-Pierog Family Foundation
items.description: The Callaghan-Pierog Family Foundation is located in Brooklyn, NY, and it usually donates to organizations that support Judaism. This includes nonprofits that cater to the needs of the Jewish community, focusing on education, cultural preservation, and religious practices. The foundation's commitment to Judaism reflects a dedication to fostering a sense of identity and community among Jewish individuals, especially through initiatives that promote the teachings and traditions of Judaism. The foundation's donations are primarily concentrated in states such as Florida and New York, indicating a strong presence in regions with significant Jewish populations. By actively supporting organizations in these areas, the foundation plays a crucial role in enhancing the community's resources and services. Overall, the foundation demonstrates a focused philanthropic approach that prioritizes the cultural and spiritual needs of the Jewish community across various states.

items.title: The John H & Cynthia Lee
items.description: The Smet Foundation is a family foundation established in 1992 by John and Cynthia Lee Smet. The foundation supports various educational initiatives and organizations in the Los Angeles area, with a particular focus on Catholic education. The foundation partners with the Department of Catholic Schools in Los Angeles to provide tuition assistance to students, as well as support for teachers, principals, and staff through programs such as Onward Readers, Onward4Educators, Onward4Excellence, Onward Leaders, Onward2HighSchool, and Onward Scholars. Additionally, the foundation supports other educational organizations in the region, including the Catholic Education Foundation, Camino Nuevo Charter Academy, The California Science Center, The Natural History Museum, and Vistamar School. The foundation does not consider unsolicited proposals and prefers to initiate projects at a small number of organizations over a sustained period of time to effect meaningful change.

items.title: James G Galanos Foundation
items.description: The James G. Galanos Foundation is located in Wilmington, DE, and it usually donates to universities. This foundation focuses its philanthropic efforts on postsecondary educational institutions that provide advanced education through postgraduate programs, catering to individuals seeking to expand their knowledge and skills at the master's or doctorate level. By supporting universities, the foundation aims to contribute to the development of higher education and the advancement of academic research. The foundation's donations have been particularly active in Pennsylvania, where it has made its contributions. With a commitment to enhancing educational opportunities, the James G. Galanos Foundation emphasizes the importance of higher learning and its role in fostering innovation and societal progress. Overall, it demonstrates a focused dedication to supporting educational institutions that prepare future leaders and professionals.

items.title: Sity Foundation
items.description: The Sity Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, focuses its donations on nonprofits dedicated to youth development, arts and culture, and educational initiatives. A significant portion of its funding supports youth services, including secondary and high schools and specialized programs for troubled youth, highlighting its commitment to fostering responsible adults. The foundation actively promotes the arts by contributing to organizations that offer diverse artistic experiences, such as music concerts, festivals, and film initiatives that enhance public appreciation of the cinematic arts. Additionally, the Sity Foundation is involved in natural resource conservation and health support, signaling its broader commitment to community well-being and environmental sustainability. It is particularly active in Washington, where it has made the most donations, along with notable contributions to California and Texas. Overall, the foundation emphasizes youth empowerment, cultural enrichment, and community health in its philanthropic efforts.

items.title: Foundation For Education & Research
items.description: The Foundation for the Education and Research in Neurological Emergencies is an independent non-profit organization dedicated to improving the care of patients with neurological emergencies. The foundation supports scientific research and provides quality medical education to emergency medical care providers, with the goal of enhancing the emergency care for neurological emergencies. The foundation has a board of directors and staff, including a president and treasurer, and is exempt from federal income tax as an organization described in section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code. Donors may deduct contributions to FERNE as provided in section 170 of the code. The foundation's mission statement outlines its principles, including the belief that patients with neurological emergencies deserve quality emergency care and that emergency medical care providers can provide optimal medical care through participation in quality medical education. The Foundation for the Education and Research in Neurological Emergencies (FERNE) is a 501c3 organization dedicated to improving the care of patients with neurological emergencies. The foundation provides educational resources, including video

items.title: Ann Jie Foundation
items.description: The Ann Jie Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, primarily donates to educational nonprofits focused on primary and secondary education. Its main support targets secondary and high schools that prepare students for college and careers, alongside universities offering postgraduate programs. The foundation also aids primary schools, including preschools and kindergartens, catering to younger students. Additionally, it provides single organization support to help specific educational institutions with fundraising. The foundation is particularly active in California and Pennsylvania, demonstrating its commitment to improving educational opportunities in these states. Overall, the Ann Jie Foundation emphasizes investment in education, highlighting the significance of academic development across various educational levels.

items.title: The G Schoenfeld Family Foundation
items.description: The g Schoenfeld Family Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, donates to various nonprofits focusing on youth development, emergency assistance, and cultural enrichment. The foundation participates in federated giving initiatives that streamline fundraising for multiple nonprofits, demonstrating its commitment to community support. It also funds organizations like museums that educate on art and science, and those dedicated to cancer research to enhance treatment and prevention. The foundation backs emergency assistance groups that provide essential resources to families in need, showcasing its dedication to alleviating hardships. Furthermore, it collaborates with advocacy groups that influence public policy, especially in legal matters, reflecting its commitment to social justice. The foundation is particularly active in California, emphasizing its focus on impactful community services.

items.title: The Hartings Family Charitable Foundation
items.description: The Hartings Family Charitable Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, primarily donates to nonprofits focused on human services, education, and religious activities. A significant portion of its funding supports educational initiatives through fundraising and distribution organizations, emphasizing the enhancement of educational opportunities. The foundation also backs human service organizations that provide essential social services to families, addressing various community needs. It prioritizes religious organizations aligned with Christianity and Protestant beliefs, fostering spiritual growth and faith-based community services. Additionally, the foundation contributes to health care, including home health care services and residential care programs for individuals needing supervision. It supports youth development programs and international relief efforts, demonstrating commitment to both local and global communities. The foundation is most active in Ohio, Florida, and Pennsylvania, while also extending its outreach to other states across the nation.

items.title: The Buddy Candler Foundation
items.description: The Buddy Candler Foundation, based in Atlanta, GA, donates to a variety of nonprofit organizations, focusing on social welfare and environmental stewardship. It supports private grantmaking foundations that are vital for funding diverse charitable activities. In addition to grantmaking, the foundation aids human service organizations that offer essential social services, including family counseling, employment assistance, and support for at-risk youth. It also emphasizes natural resource conservation, underscoring its commitment to environmental sustainability for future generations. The foundation is particularly active in Georgia, where it has made the majority of its donations, followed by Tennessee and Louisiana, demonstrating a regional focus on supporting local communities and their specific social and environmental challenges.

items.title: Crossroads Foundation
items.description: The Crossroads Foundation is a non-profit organization established in November 2018 with the mission of serving underserved communities by addressing concerns around youth education, homelessness, poverty, and mental health services. The foundation aims to achieve this through developing community services, offering resources for education and guidance, identifying and responding to the needs of the homeless and undernourished communities, and raising awareness about social issues with the youth and uninformed. The board of directors includes Eric Keyes as president, Lee Langon as vice president and sponsorship director, Vallorie O'Neil as treasurer, Joyce Johnson as secretary, and Dr. Benita Anderson as fundraising consultant. The foundation has shifted its focus from a home scholarship program to charitable activities, events, and sponsorships. Donation and fundraising information is available through the organization.

items.title: Lig Global Foundation INC
items.description: The Lig Global Foundation Inc., based in Teaneck, NJ, focuses on donating to organizations that support international relief efforts and alleviate poverty in developing countries. It provides essential resources such as funds, technical assistance, and supplies to enhance health, education, welfare, and social well-being for individuals and families in need. The foundation also aids organizations responding to major disasters and emergencies abroad, underscoring its commitment to humanitarian assistance. Its donation activities primarily target states like Texas and Virginia, with additional contributions in Colorado and Illinois. Overall, the Lig Global Foundation actively supports international relief initiatives, reflecting its mission to promote self-reliance and improve the quality of life for vulnerable communities worldwide.

items.title: The Tim And Leah Chapman Foundation
items.description: The Tim and Leah Chapman Foundation, based in Ponte Vedra, FL, supports a variety of nonprofit organizations primarily focused on health care, education, and social services. Its main donation areas emphasize community welfare, particularly through ambulatory and primary health care services for outpatient treatment. The foundation also backs personal social services and federated giving programs that facilitate fundraising for multiple causes. Education is another priority, with contributions to secondary, high, primary, and elementary institutions, showcasing a commitment to youth development. The foundation promotes specific services like counseling and youth programs to encourage personal growth among young people. Additionally, it addresses animal welfare and reproductive health, reflecting a comprehensive philanthropic vision. The foundation is particularly active in Georgia and Florida, underscoring its regional commitment to these important causes.

items.title: Lighten Family Foundation
items.description: The Lighten Family Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, primarily supports nonprofits focused on education, community support, and public safety. A significant portion of their donations goes towards scholarships and financial aid, helping students in need access educational resources. The foundation also backs law enforcement organizations that enhance community safety and support police officers. Additionally, they contribute to Boys & Girls Clubs, which provide essential services and activities for youth, especially from disadvantaged backgrounds, promoting character and leadership skills. Other focus areas include special education services for children with disabilities, legal aid for economically disadvantaged individuals, and community-based neighborhood centers offering diverse programs. The foundation's charitable efforts are mainly concentrated in New York and Tennessee, demonstrating a commitment to making a positive impact in these states.

items.title: Merrion Family Foundation
items.description: The Merrion Family Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, primarily donates to nonprofit organizations focused on youth development and community support. A significant portion of its funding goes to Youth Development Programs, which provide recreational and educational opportunities for children and young adults, fostering responsible and productive citizens. The foundation also supports Community Foundations that offer grants for local charitable initiatives and organizations dedicated to Economic Development that stimulate local economies and create job opportunities. Additionally, it contributes to Animal Protection and Welfare organizations and Christian groups focused on spiritual growth and community worship. The foundation has a strong presence in New Mexico and Colorado, supporting programs addressing public health issues like Reproductive Health Care and Substance Abuse Prevention. Through its diverse engagement, the Merrion Family Foundation is committed to enhancing the well-being of individuals and communities across various states.

items.title: The Muskin Family Foundation
items.description: The Muskin Family Foundation, based in Boston, MA, primarily donates to nonprofits focused on education, community services, and rights advocacy. It significantly supports Developmentally Disabled Centers, which provide crucial services for individuals with disabilities, and prioritizes funding for Primary and Elementary Schools, including preschool and kindergarten programs. The foundation also champions free speech by backing organizations that promote press freedom and combat censorship. In addition, it provides assistance to Food Banks and Pantries, reinforcing its commitment to human services. Active mainly in Massachusetts, Ohio, and Connecticut, the foundation extends its efforts to states like California and Alabama as well. Ultimately, the Muskin Family Foundation seeks to enhance educational opportunities and support vulnerable communities through its varied contributions.

items.title: Frederick Banting Foundation INC
items.description: The Frederick Banting Foundation is a non-profit organization that supports research for a cure or improvement of the lives of those with Juvenile Diabetes, also known as Type 1 Diabetes. The foundation was inspired by Dr. Frederick Banting's vision and persistence in discovering insulin and improving the lives of diabetic patients. The foundation provides research funding to scientists and medical professionals who share Banting's vision and tenacity. The foundation's mission is to "support researchers working to cure or improve the lives of those with Juvenile Diabetes." They believe that by investing in research, they can make a significant impact on the lives of diabetic patients and potentially find a cure for this disease. Overall, the Frederick Banting Foundation is dedicated to finding a cure or improving the lives of those with Juvenile Diabetes through research and fundraising efforts. The Frederick Banting Foundation is a non-profit organization that supports research for a cure or improvement of the lives of those with Juvenile Diabetes. The foundation was established in honor of Sir Frederick G. Banting, the Nobel Prize winner in 1923 for the discovery of insulin. The

items.title: George & Irina Schaeffer Foundation
items.description: George W. Schaeffer is a successful entrepreneur and philanthropist who has founded several companies, including OPI Products and People's Trust Insurance. Through his foundation, the George and Irina Schaeffer Foundation, he has donated millions of dollars to charities worldwide, including the American Heart Association, the Leukemia Research Foundation, and the National Lung Association. Schaeffer is also actively involved in the Bnai Zion Foundation, which supports critical health and education projects in Israel. In addition to his philanthropic work, Schaeffer has received numerous awards for his entrepreneurial achievements, including the Spirit of Life Award from the City of Hope Cancer Center.

items.title: The Anne R Monroe Foundation
items.description: The Anne R. Monroe Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, donates to various nonprofit organizations focused on community welfare. A significant portion of its funding supports military and veterans' organizations, showcasing a commitment to those who have served. The foundation also prioritizes animal welfare, contributing to shelters and initiatives for humane treatment of animals. In addition, it funds human service organizations that provide vital social services, including counseling and employment assistance. The foundation supports centers for the blind and visually impaired, promoting inclusivity for individuals with disabilities, and backs rehabilitative care organizations that help restore independence. Scholarships and student financial aid are also emphasized, reflecting a commitment to education. The foundation primarily operates in Pennsylvania, followed by New York and Florida, focusing its efforts where it can make a significant impact. Overall, the Anne R. Monroe Foundation is dedicated to enhancing the lives of individuals and communities across multiple states.

items.title: Pennoni Family Foundation
items.description: The Pennoni Family Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, primarily supports educational and community-focused nonprofits, with a strong emphasis on higher education. The Companion Animal Parasite Council (CAPC) is a non-profit organization that provides guidelines and resources for the prevention and control of parasites in animals, including dogs, cats, and humans. This lack of engagement with well-known causes raises questions about the foundation's mission and objectives, as it appears to operate without a clear commitment to addressing pressing social issues or supporting established nonprofit organizations that are recognized for their impactful work.

items.title: Foster Family Charitable Foundation
items.description: The Foster Family Foundation is a non-profit organization that provides equal access to opportunities, programs, and resources for individuals living in marginalized and under-resourced communities. The foundation was established by Glenn Foster Jr. and his wife Pamela Foster. The foundation supports programs for youth fulfilling their dreams.

items.title: Richard Grand Foundation
items.description: The Richard Grand Foundation is located in San Francisco, CA, and donates to nonprofits located in various states across the United States. Despite its active philanthropic efforts, the foundation does not appear to support any widely recognized causes or initiatives. Instead, its donations are distributed among numerous organizations without a clear focus on specific areas such as education, health, or social justice. In total, the foundation has donated to nonprofits in 12 unique states, with the highest number of donations concentrated in California and Arizona. This distribution indicates a preference for local organizations, yet the lack of alignment with known causes raises questions about the overall impact and intentions of the foundation's charitable giving. The foundation's approach suggests a desire to support local nonprofits, but it is unclear how these donations translate into meaningful contributions to societal challenges.

items.title: Shapell Manuscript Foundation
items.description: The Shapell Manuscript Foundation is an independent educational organization dedicated to collecting and exhibiting original manuscripts and historical documents, with a focus on American history and the Holy Land in the 19th and 20th centuries. The foundation's holdings include items from notable figures such as Abraham Lincoln, Mark Twain, Albert Einstein, and Theodor Herzl, as well as a large collection of Jewish soldiers who served in the Civil War. The foundation also includes diverse subjects such as Custer and the Little Big Horn. The manuscripts provide unique insights into aspects of American history and culture, and the foundation offers educational resources and exhibitions for the public to explore.

items.title: The H Scott Caven Iii Memorial Foundation
items.description: The H. Scott Caven III Memorial Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, primarily supports nonprofits aiding individuals with disabilities, low-income families, and educational institutions. Its donations mainly focus on group homes, ensuring 24-hour non-medical care for individuals with disabilities. The foundation also backs housing development organizations that create affordable options for low-income families, including those with disabilities. Additionally, it supports alumni associations that connect graduates with resources and funds legal services for economically disadvantaged individuals. The foundation prioritizes aid for patients and their families, donating to organizations that provide essential support and recreational activities for those facing serious health challenges. Active primarily in Texas and Virginia, the foundation demonstrates a commitment to serving diverse communities across the nation.

items.title: Galvan Foundation Charitable Trust
items.description: The Galvan Foundation Charitable Trust, based in New York, NY, supports a wide range of nonprofit organizations, primarily focusing on arts and culture initiatives that enhance access to visual and performing arts and theater productions. It also funds human service organizations providing essential social services like family counseling and employment assistance. Additionally, the foundation contributes to housing rehabilitation projects that revitalize neighborhoods by restoring old structures and clearing slums. Environmental initiatives include donations to organizations focused on water resources and wetlands conservation. The foundation also backs historical societies for cultural heritage preservation, youth development programs, and research institutes analyzing public policy. While it is particularly active in New York, the foundation also directs significant donations to states like Georgia and Colorado, among others.

items.title: The Ted Muhs Foundation
items.description: The Ted Muhs Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, primarily supports nonprofit organizations in health care, community improvement, and education. A significant portion of its donations goes to general hospitals, which provide essential health services and emergency care. The foundation also funds community service clubs that enhance local life through various projects. Additionally, it supports public health organizations that educate on health hazards and promote early detection of illnesses. Higher education institutions receive backing to help individuals acquire advanced knowledge and skills. The foundation is also involved in eye disease research to improve treatment options for vision impairments, underscoring its commitment to health causes. Geographically, the foundation is most active in California, Nevada, and New York, reflecting a broad reach across the U.S. Overall, the Ted Muhs Foundation is dedicated to enhancing health, education, and community welfare.

items.title: The Parsekian Foundation INC
items.description: The Parsekian Foundation Inc., based in South Boston, MA, primarily supports organizations focused on international development, education, and cultural promotion. A significant portion of its funding enhances the capacity of nations outside the U.S. in areas such as health, education, and agriculture. The foundation also invests in graduate and professional education, aiding those who have completed their undergraduate studies. It champions cultural and ethnic awareness initiatives that preserve diverse traditions and promote community understanding. Additional funding areas include international agricultural development to boost food security and productivity in developing countries, as well as promoting philanthropy to encourage charitable giving and volunteerism. The foundation is particularly active in Massachusetts, Washington D.C., and California, demonstrating its commitment to both local and global causes.

items.title: Godley Family Foundation
items.description: The Godley Family Foundation, based in N Kingstown, RI, donates to a variety of nonprofits, focusing on international and public health issues. A significant portion of their contributions aids international relief efforts, targeting poverty alleviation and essential services in developing countries. The foundation prioritizes public health initiatives, funding organizations that enhance awareness and healthcare access for those with illnesses or disabilities. Moreover, it invests in international development and agricultural projects to foster sustainable communities through education and resources. The foundation also supports scholarships and financial aid programs to help students in need pursue their education. Active in states like New York and Massachusetts, the Godley Family Foundation is dedicated to improving both local and global communities through its philanthropic activities.

items.title: Halpryn Family Foundation INC
items.description: The Halpryn Family Foundation Inc., based in Miami, FL, primarily supports organizations focused on science and technology education, particularly science and technology museums that develop educational exhibits related to mathematics and physical sciences. This commitment reflects the foundation's goal of fostering public understanding and appreciation of scientific principles. Active mainly in Florida, the foundation has made numerous donations to local initiatives, aiming to inspire future generations in STEM (science, technology, engineering, and mathematics) fields. By focusing on educational institutions that promote science and technology, the Halpryn Family Foundation seeks to enrich knowledge and curiosity in these essential areas, thereby positively impacting local communities and enhancing educational opportunities.

items.title: Eisele Family Foundation
items.description: The Eisele Family Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, primarily donates to organizations focused on education, youth development, and community services. A significant portion of its funding supports scholarships and financial aid for students in need, particularly at undergraduate colleges offering bachelor’s degrees. The foundation also emphasizes youth services, contributing to organizations that assist children and youth in vulnerable situations, including girls clubs that promote character and leadership. Moreover, the foundation funds initiatives related to religion and community engagement, such as local radio productions. While its donations are particularly active in states like Colorado and Nebraska, the foundation maintains a diverse reach across various regions. Overall, the Eisele Family Foundation is dedicated to fostering educational, cultural, and social welfare causes, reflecting its commitment to community growth and individual development.

items.title: James F Cooper And Jean M Cooper
items.description: The James F. Cooper and Jean M. Cooper Foundation is located in Chanute, KS, and it usually donates to nonprofits that focus on youth engagement and support for families in crisis. The foundation's primary emphasis is on organizations that operate youth centers and clubs, which aim to provide supervised recreational and social activities for children and youth, particularly those from disadvantaged backgrounds. These centers play a vital role in fostering character development and leadership skills among young participants. Additionally, the foundation supports family violence shelters, which offer critical temporary housing and assistance for women and their children who have experienced domestic violence. The foundation is most active in Kansas, reflecting its commitment to addressing local community needs through targeted support in these vital areas. Overall, its philanthropic efforts are concentrated on enhancing the well-being of children and families facing challenges.

items.title: Jolley Family Foundation
items.description: The Jolley Foundation is a philanthropic organization based in Greenville, South Carolina that supports community initiatives and organizations working to advance equity and combat systems and social structures perpetuating poverty and discrimination. The foundation provides funding and resources to local partners, including GirlUp GVL, USC Upstate Foundation, and Mill Community Ministries, among others. The Jolley Foundation's mission is to empower those with proximity to and lived experience with inequity to effect change, invest in advocacy, policy change, and movement-building, and build capacity, leadership, and innovation within systemically under-resourced populations. The foundation uses various forms of philanthropic capital, including social, moral, intellectual, reputational, financial, and trustee expertise to combat these systems and structures. The Jolley Foundation was founded almost 80 years ago by husband and wife Bob and Rucker Jolley as an employee assistance fund and has been largely family-run since its beginning, but the community's needs have always been the heart of their work. In recent years, the foundation has updated its funding strategies to better alig

items.title: Dallas County Farmers Foundation
items.description: The Dallas County Foundation is a charitable organization that supports various causes and programs in the Dallas County area. In 2025, the foundation awarded grants to 36 organizations, totaling $200,000 in funds. The grant recipients included schools, libraries, community centers, and other non-profit organizations that focused on areas such as health, human services, arts & culture, historic preservation, and environment. The foundation accepts grant applications annually and has a team of professional advisors who help donors align their passions with planning. Overall, the Dallas County Foundation is dedicated to improving the quality of life in the communities it serves.

items.title: The Martin Wong Foundation
items.description: The Martin Wong Foundation is a non-profit organization established to support young artists and commemorate the life and achievements of Martin Wong. The foundation provides art scholarships to selected universities and supports an Art Education program centered in New York. The scholarship competition was established to recognize and support excellence in ceramics and painting, the areas in which Martin Wong first pursued his interest in art. For 2025, the Foundation will offer three scholarships in painting/2D media and three scholarships in ceramics/3D media at each participating institution, with prizes ranging from $5,000 to $2,000. To be eligible, students must be enrolled at one of the four universities affiliated with the Foundation. The Martin Wong Foundation is a non-profit organization established to support and celebrate the life and achievements of Martin Wong, an artist who passed away in 1999. The foundation provides art scholarships to selected universities and supports an Art Education program centered in New York. The scholarship competition was established to recognize and support excellence in ceramics and painting, the areas in which Martin Wong first pursued

items.title: Father John Foundation
items.description: Father John W. Scannell was a Catholic priest who founded the foundation under God. The foundation supports various causes such as confession, mass attendance, and receiving the Eucharist. Father Scannell believed that everyone is a hobo and their true home is in heaven, emphasizing the importance of eternal life through Jesus Christ. The website comments section was copyrighted by Sun-Pro of California, Inc., with terms of use and in place.

items.title: The Alvin Marley Foundation INC
items.description: The Alvin Marley Foundation Inc, based in Wilmington, DE, donates to various nonprofit organizations focused on education, arts, and community services. It particularly supports initiatives that raise funds for specific educational institutions, enhancing educational opportunities. The foundation also backs professional societies that promote collaboration and networking among vocational fields. In addition to education, it invests in the arts, especially theater and film, reflecting a commitment to cultural enrichment. Donations to young men’s and women’s associations emphasize community-building and youth engagement. The foundation is most active in Tennessee, Illinois, and Missouri, while also supporting organizations in states like California and New York. This broad engagement demonstrates its dedication to a wide range of causes, showcasing a commitment to improving educational, cultural, and community outcomes.

items.title: The Peternell Family Foundation
items.description: The Peternell Family Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, supports a variety of nonprofit organizations focused on education, community support, and environmental conservation. A notable portion of its funding is directed towards specific educational institutions, reflecting its commitment to fostering learning and development. The foundation also emphasizes adult education programs that enhance skills and knowledge beyond traditional schooling and invests in land conservation to protect natural areas for future generations. Additionally, it supports camps that provide outdoor experiences and community foundations that offer grants for local initiatives. Active particularly in Colorado, the foundation demonstrates a strong commitment to addressing both local and broader community needs across multiple states while prioritizing education, environmental stewardship, and community enhancement.

items.title: Tessier Family Foundation INC
items.description: The Tessier Family Foundation Inc., based in Pierre, SD, primarily donates to nonprofits focused on education and community service, with a significant emphasis on children's service agencies that enhance child welfare. The foundation also funds public foundations that engage in grantmaking for social, educational, and charitable purposes. Additionally, it supports undergraduate colleges, crucial for providing higher education and fostering student growth. The foundation's philanthropic efforts are mainly concentrated in South Dakota, where it has made many donations, demonstrating its commitment to improving the local community through educational and social initiatives. Overall, the Tessier Family Foundation is dedicated to promoting education and the well-being of children in its active regions.

items.title: The Page Foundation
items.description: The Page Education Foundation is a non-profit organization that supports and inspires young people of color in Minnesota to pursue post-secondary education. The foundation was established in 1988 by Diane and Alan Page, who were committed to encouraging and motivating students of color to succeed academically. The foundation provides financial resources and guidance to help students navigate their higher education journey, as well as fosters positive mentor relationships and role models for children. Since its inception, the foundation has awarded over $16 million in grants to more than 8,000 Page Scholars, who have volunteered nearly 500,000 hours working with 50,000 children across Minnesota. The foundation relies on corporations, foundations, and individuals for 100% of its funding, and donations are used to support academic programs, service to children, and mentorship opportunities.

items.title: Charles H Dishman Iii Family Foundation INC
items.description: The Charles H. Dishman III Family Foundation Inc., based in Wilmington, DE, supports a wide array of nonprofit organizations. Primarily, it funds art museums dedicated to preserving and showcasing valuable collections, thus enhancing cultural appreciation. The foundation also contributes to libraries that promote community education and access to resources. In education, it backs organizations that provide essential services to help students succeed. Health initiatives are another focus, with investments in research for diseases affecting specific organs and mental health disorders. Additionally, the foundation is committed to environmental causes, supporting conservation and forest protection efforts. Its activities are particularly concentrated in Kentucky, Florida, and North Carolina, reflecting a commitment to both local and broader nonprofit initiatives in these regions.

items.title: Gobioff Foundation
items.description: The foundation, established in 2007 by Howard Gobioff, supports human rights organizations and the Tampa arts community through grants. The directive given by Mr. Gobioff is to "make the world a better place." The foundation has two primary funding areas: the arts and human rights/civil liberties. Small grants are provided to enhance the cultural landscape of the Tampa Bay area through cross-sector and community partnerships. Specific causes supported by the foundation include poetry festivals, holiday events for the LGBT+ community, and publicly accessible art projects. The foundation also funds specific programs or initiatives such as the Treasure Tampa microgrant program, State Flower Ladies event, Temps Projects Ground4Art @ HCC, Darkroom Silver Linings project, and Bird of Paradise open studio tour.

items.title: Shree Chakradhar Charitable
items.description: The Shree Chakradhar Charitable Foundation, based in Valrico, FL, primarily donates to nonprofits that promote international understanding by fostering appreciation for diverse cultures through events, forums, and exchanges. By supporting such initiatives, the foundation enhances cross-cultural dialogue and collaboration, bridging gaps between societies. Geographically, its contributions are concentrated in North Carolina, New York, and Florida, with additional support in California and other regions. This focus reflects the foundation's commitment to enhancing cultural awareness and cooperation. Overall, the Shree Chakradhar Charitable Foundation plays a vital role in encouraging global connectivity through its targeted philanthropic efforts.

items.title: The Barbara And Stephen Miller Foundation
items.description: The Barbara and Stephen Miller Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, donates to a variety of nonprofit organizations, emphasizing private grantmaking and independent foundations that provide financial support for social, educational, and charitable initiatives. It significantly contributes to history museums, aiding in the preservation of cultural heritage. The foundation also invests in scholarships and financial aid for students, ensuring access to education for those in need. Additional support extends to arts, culture, and music organizations that foster creative expression and community involvement. Moreover, it backs community and neighborhood development initiatives aimed at improving local social and economic conditions. The foundation is particularly active in Missouri and Illinois, demonstrating its commitment to creating a meaningful impact in these areas and beyond.

items.title: Francis And Christine Martin Family
items.description: The Martin Family Foundation is a family foundation established in 1991 to honor the lives of Florence MacFarlane Martin, Edward Thomas Martin, and E. Thomas Martin. The foundation supports causes related to education, environmental and animal conservation, and mental and physical health, with a particular focus on empowering women and children, protecting the environment, and helping underserved communities. The foundation members include Annie Bowler, Julie Martin, Laura Martin Sherlock, Riley Sherlock, Nicole Bowler, Devin Corea, Wyatt Westfall, and Meghan Martin. The foundation is inspired by the values of their loved ones, including an entrepreneurial spirit, a desire to make peace between individuals, and a drive for continuous learning. They also support organizations through grant proposals and volunteering/traveling.

items.title: Pinon Trail Foundation
items.description: The Pinon Trail Foundation, based in Denver, CO, donates to various nonprofit organizations focused on community development. Its primary contributions are directed towards Chambers of Commerce and Business Leagues that enhance local economic vitality. The foundation also supports Public Foundations that engage in grantmaking for social, educational, and charitable purposes, promoting community welfare. Additionally, it invests in higher education by donating to universities and Community Foundations that provide regional grants for charitable initiatives. The foundation is involved in efforts to prevent domestic violence, support single parents, and aid the homeless. While its primary focus is on Colorado, the foundation also extends its philanthropy to states like New York, Michigan, and Texas, showcasing a commitment to broader community impact. Overall, the Pinon Trail Foundation is dedicated to enhancing education, community services, and economic development through its contributions.

items.title: Williams Family Private Foundation
items.description: The Williams Family Foundation is a non-profit organization established in 2017 to provide grants to qualified 501(c)(3) organizations that serve various causes, including animal welfare, education, and protection of vulnerable adults. The foundation only donates to established organizations and prioritizes supporting local charities that provide shelter, food, guidance, and counseling to at-risk parents, children, and young adults. The foundation has been actively involved in social justice, Christian organizations, and animal welfare causes and has held fundraising events to support these initiatives. The Foundation's primary goal is to enhance infrastructure, programs, and outreach of selected organizations through financial support.

items.title: Calvin And Kelly Carter Family Foundation
items.description: The Calvin and Kelly Carter Family Foundation, based in Wilmington, DE, primarily donates to nonprofit organizations focused on human services. Its major contributions support Human Service Organizations that provide vital social services such as family counseling and employment assistance. The foundation also aids Patient & Family Support organizations that enhance the quality of life for families dealing with illness. Additionally, it contributes to Christianity-focused groups that promote spiritual growth and community involvement. Emergency Assistance programs, including food banks and shelters, receive substantial support, underscoring the foundation's commitment to alleviating hardships faced by vulnerable populations. While it has a strong regional focus in North Carolina, the foundation also supports various initiatives across multiple states, reflecting its dedication to addressing diverse social needs.

items.title: Birdseye Foundation
items.description: The Birdseye Foundation, based in Lebanon, NH, focuses on supporting nonprofits that enhance community resources and youth development. A major part of its donations goes to Land Resources Conservation, helping organizations protect endangered lands and manage natural resources sustainably, showcasing its commitment to environmental stewardship. The foundation also invests in Primary & Elementary Schools, ensuring young children receive quality education and foundational experiences, alongside broader educational initiatives through its support for Education N.E.C. Youth Development Programs are another priority, as the foundation aims to foster engaged and responsible youth through recreational and cultural activities. Additionally, it addresses immediate needs by donating to Emergency Assistance organizations, aiding families in hardship. The foundation is particularly active in Vermont and New Hampshire, contributing significantly to these important causes.
